title: Capstone 13 — MCP Server with Registry and Governance

questions.question: Which authorization model gates tool calls in this capstone?
options: OAuth 2.1 tokens carrying per-tool scopes, checked at tool-call time
A shared admin password
API keys per IP address
Mutual TLS without scopes
questions.explanation: OAuth 2.1 tokens carry per-tool scopes like `jira:read`, `s3:list`, and `postgres:query:readonly`, which the MCP server checks at tool-call time rather than just at session start.

questions.question: What is the role of the .well-known/mcp-capabilities document?
options: Stores audit logs for the server
Lists outbound IP ranges
Holds the OPA policy bundle
Exposes the tool manifest, transport URL, and auth requirements so the registry can validate and index the server
questions.explanation: The .well-known/mcp-capabilities document exposes the tool manifest, transport URL, and auth requirements for service discovery and client auto-configuration.

questions.question: Why do destructive tools live on a separate MCP server in this design?
options: They cannot be exposed over StreamableHTTP
They run on slower hardware
They are gated behind an approval token elevated via a Slack card within a short window
They require a different programming language
questions.explanation: The registry stores tool definitions with versioned schemas, enabling clients to discover available tools, understand their parameters, and fetch compatible versions.

questions.question: What does OPA / Rego decide on every tool call?
options: Which model to invoke
Where to write the audit log
How the server should re-rank the response
Whether the caller's scopes permit invocation, plus PII redaction and payload caps
questions.explanation: Governance uses OPA policies to enforce rules: rate limits per tool, allowed operations (read-only vs read-write), and access control based on user roles and scopes.

questions.question: Which evidence demonstrates StreamableHTTP horizontal scaling in the load test?
options: Reducing concurrency to one client
Switching to stdio transport
Running everything in a single process
Adding a second replica and showing the load balancer redistributing without session stickiness
questions.explanation: The capstone measures registration accuracy (correctness of tool schemas), governance enforcement effectiveness (percentage of policy violations blocked), and service discovery reliability.

questions.question: Why does the audit log scrub PII via Presidio before being persisted per tenant?
options: To make logs shorter
To meet enterprise security requirements while keeping per-call lineage queryable
Presidio reduces ClickHouse write amplification
PII improves search performance
questions.explanation: Security is ensured by TLS encryption for all communications, signed JWTs for OAuth tokens, and regular security audits of the OPA policy bundle.
